speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: You’re well met once again.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: So are you.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: You come to take your stand here and behold

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The Lady Anne pass from her coronation?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis all my business. At our last encounter,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The Duke of Buckingham came from his trial.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis very true. But that time offered sorrow,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: This general joy.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis well. The citizens

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I am sure have shown at full their royal minds,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: As, let ’em have their rights, they are ever forward

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: In celebration of this day with shows,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Pageants, and sights of honor.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Never greater,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Nor, I’ll assure you, better taken, sir.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: May I be bold to ask what that contains,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That paper in your hand?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Yes, ’tis the list

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Of those that claim their offices this day

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: By custom of the coronation.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The Duke of Suffolk is the first, and claims

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: To be High Steward; next, the Duke of Norfolk,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: He to be Earl Marshal. You may read the rest.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I thank you, sir. Had I not known those customs,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I should have been beholding to your paper.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: But I beseech you, what’s become of Katherine,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The Princess Dowager? How goes her business?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That I can tell you too. The Archbishop

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Of Canterbury, accompanied with other

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Learnèd and reverend fathers of his order,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Held a late court at Dunstable, six miles off

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: From Ampthill, where the Princess lay, to which

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: She was often cited by them, but appeared not;

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And, to be short, for not appearance and

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The King’s late scruple, by the main assent

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Of all these learnèd men she was divorced,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And the late marriage made of none effect;

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Since which she was removed to Kymmalton,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Where she remains now sick.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Alas, good lady!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The trumpets sound. Stand close. The Queen is coming.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: A royal train, believe me! These I know.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Who’s that that bears the scepter?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Marques Dorset,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And that the Earl of Surrey with the rod.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: A bold brave gentleman.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That should be

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The Duke of Suffolk.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis the same: High Steward.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And that my Lord of Norfolk?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Yes.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Heaven bless thee!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Thou hast the sweetest face I ever looked on.—

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Sir, as I have a soul, she is an angel.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Our king has all the Indies in his arms,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And more, and richer, when he strains that lady.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I cannot blame his conscience.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: They that bear

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The cloth of honor over her are four barons

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Of the Cinque-ports.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Those men are happy, and so are all are near her.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I take it she that carries up the train

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Is that old noble lady, Duchess of Norfolk.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: It is, and all the rest are countesses.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Their coronets say so. These are stars indeed.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And sometimes falling ones.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: No more of that.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: God save you, sir. Where have you been broiling?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Among the crowd i’ th’ Abbey, where a finger

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Could not be wedged in more. I am stifled

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: With the mere rankness of their joy.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: You saw

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The ceremony?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That I did.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: How was it?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Well worth the seeing.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Good sir, speak it to us!

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: As well as I am able. The rich stream

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Of lords and ladies, having brought the Queen

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: To a prepared place in the choir, fell off

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: A distance from her, while her Grace sat down

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: To rest awhile, some half an hour or so,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: In a rich chair of state, opposing freely

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The beauty of her person to the people.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Believe me, sir, she is the goodliest woman

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That ever lay by man, which when the people

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Had the full view of, such a noise arose

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: As the shrouds make at sea in a stiff tempest—

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: As loud and to as many tunes. Hats, cloaks,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Doublets, I think, flew up, and had their faces

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Been loose, this day they had been lost. Such joy

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I never saw before. Great-bellied women

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That had not half a week to go, like rams

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: In the old time of war, would shake the press

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And make ’em reel before ’em. No man living

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Could say “This is my wife there,” all were woven

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: So strangely in one piece.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: But what followed?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: At length her Grace rose, and with modest paces

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Came to the altar, where she kneeled and saintlike

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Cast her fair eyes to heaven and prayed devoutly,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Then rose again and bowed her to the people.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: When by the Archbishop of Canterbury

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: She had all the royal makings of a queen—

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: As, holy oil, Edward Confessor’s crown,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The rod, and bird of peace, and all such emblems—

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Laid nobly on her; which performed, the choir,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: With all the choicest music of the kingdom,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Together sung Te Deum. So she parted,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And with the same full state paced back again

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: To York Place, where the feast is held.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Sir,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: You must no more call it “York Place”; that’s past,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: For since the Cardinal fell, that title’s lost.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis now the King’s and called “Whitehall.”

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I know it,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: But ’tis so lately altered that the old name

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Is fresh about me.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: What two reverend bishops

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Were those that went on each side of the Queen?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Stokeley and Gardiner, the one of Winchester,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Newly preferred from the King’s secretary,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The other London.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: He of Winchester

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Is held no great good lover of the Archbishop’s,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The virtuous Cranmer.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: All the land knows that.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: However, yet there is no great breach. When it comes,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Cranmer will find a friend will not shrink from him.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Who may that be, I pray you?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Thomas Cromwell,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: A man in much esteem with th’ King, and truly

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: A worthy friend. The King has made him

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Master o’ th’ Jewel House,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And one already of the Privy Council.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: He will deserve more.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Yes, without all doubt.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Come, gentlemen, you shall go my way,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Which is to th’ court, and there you shall be my guests,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Something I can command. As I walk thither,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I’ll tell you more.

speaker: BOTH
text: You may command us, sir.
